prompt_text: Is Salesflare better than HubSpot for track email opens and follow-ups?
response:
For **purely tracking email opens and managing follow-ups**, **Salesflare is generally better than HubSpot for small–mid B2B teams**, mainly because its tracking is deeper, less limited, and more focused on sales workflows, while HubSpot is stronger as an all‑in‑one platform rather than as a specialized tracker.[1][2]

Here is the comparison focused on **email opens & follow-ups**:

| Aspect | **Salesflare** | **HubSpot** |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Email open tracking** | Yes, unlimited opens, integrated into a sales-focused CRM.[1][5] | Yes, but free version limits tracking notifications (200/month).[2] |
| **Click tracking** | Yes, tracks **clicks and website visits** automatically on its Growth plan, no extra add-ons.[1][2] | Yes, click tracking; website visits tracking may require add-ons / higher tiers.[1] |
| **Follow-up reminders & sequences** | Built‑in **automated email sequences**, follow-up reminders, and workflows specifically for B2B sales follow-ups.[2][3] | Sequences and automation available in Sales Hub, but more paywalled and part of a broader sales/marketing suite.[1][2] |
| **Real-time alerts** | Real-time alerts for opens and clicks via browser/app, with no explicit volume cap noted.[1][5] | Real-time notifications for opens/clicks, but free tier caps total tracking notifications.[1][2] |
| **Pricing vs tracking depth** | Growth plan (~$29/user/mo) includes opens, clicks, and website visits with **no hidden limits**, optimized for <20 reps.[1] | Free CRM includes basic tracking; meaningful, scalable use typically requires paid Sales Hub and can get relatively expensive.[1][2] |
| **Gmail & Outlook support** | Works with both Gmail and Outlook.[2] | Also integrates with both Gmail and Outlook via extensions.[1][2] |
| **Primary strength** | **Best overall CRM for email tracking** depth and automation for small–mid B2B teams.[1][2] | **Best all‑in‑one platform** (CRM + marketing + sales), but “not the strongest for tracking specifically.”[1] |

Key points relevant to your question:

- A 2026 comparison of CRMs with email tracking ranks **Salesflare as “Best overall”** for tracking emails, links, and website visits automatically, explicitly noting that if **tracking depth is your primary concern, Salesflare gives you more signal per dollar** than tools like HubSpot.[1]  
- A 2026 email tracker ranking gives **Salesflare a 9.9/10** vs **HubSpot Sales Hub 8.4/10**, calling Salesflare’s email tracker **“the most advanced one in this list”**, with strong deliverability and advanced exclusion of your own opens, plus integrated website tracking and notifications.[2]  
- HubSpot’s free tracking is solid but **limited**: its free tier “tracks only email opens” and uniquely **limits tracking notifications to about 200 per month**, after which you must upgrade at a relatively high price.[2]  
- Salesflare’s Growth plan bundles **email tracking, link click tracking, and website visit tracking** without extra charges or hidden limits, explicitly positioned as the best tracking-to-price ratio for small and mid-size sales teams.[1]

So:

- If your priority is **accurate, rich engagement tracking (opens, clicks, site visits) and automated follow-ups** in a lean, sales-focused CRM, **Salesflare is usually the better choice**.  
- If you mainly want **a broad ecosystem** (marketing automation, CMS, service, etc.) and email tracking is just one piece, **HubSpot may be a better overall platform**, but **not better specifically for tracking opens and follow-ups**.
